pattern: "Maximize the minimum gap" is monotonic in the candidate gap size -- if a gap of d lets you fit m balls, any smaller gap also does -- so binary search the answer and check feasibility with a greedy placement.
hints: Sort the basket positions first -- the placement that maximizes the minimum spacing always places balls in position order.

For a candidate minimum gap d, greedily place a ball in the first basket, then walk forward placing the next ball in the first basket that is at least d away from the last placed one. Count how many balls fit.

Whether m balls fit is monotonic in d (a smaller d always fits at least as many balls as a larger one) -- binary search on d between 1 and the full span, using that feasibility check to narrow the range.

edge_inputs.description: unsorted input with a huge span
minimum baskets, minimum balls -- must use both
m equals the number of baskets, every basket must be used
evenly spaced baskets, the optimal gap is exactly the spacing
baskets clustered together plus one far outlier
two baskets only, forced to use both

stress.description: random distinct basket positions with a random ball count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 50000))
    positions = rng.sample(range(1, 2 * n + 1), n)
    m = rng.randint(2, n)
    return [positions, m]
